page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the activities of daily living (ADL) scales, specifically focusing on the ability to engage in bathing across three distinct phases of patient care: the Pre-Fracture Phase, Ward Admission Phase, and Post-Discharge Phase. During the Pre-Fracture Phase, a total of 129 individuals were assessed, revealing critical insights into their baseline functional capabilities. The subsequent Ward Admission Phase maintained the same sample size, allowing for a direct comparison of how acute medical conditions may impact the ability to perform essential tasks such as bathing. Notably, the transition to the Post-Discharge Phase, which included 89 participants, provides valuable data on recovery trajectories and the effectiveness of rehabilitation efforts in restoring independence in daily activities.

page.content: By analyzing the percentage of patients able to bathe independently across these phases, we can better understand the implications of fractures on functional mobility and the necessary interventions required during the continuum of care. This data is vital for healthcare professionals aiming to enhance patient outcomes and develop tailored rehabilitation programs that address the specific needs of individuals recovering from fractures.

implications of fractures on functional mobility and the necessary interventions required during the continuum of care. This data
